running_combat.title: Running Combat
running_combat.overview: Tips for keeping the game running smoothly when a fight breaks out
running_combat.note: Builds on combat rules in Playing the Game

rolling_initiative.title: Rolling Initiative
rolling_initiative.core_principle: Combat starts when—and only when—you (the DM) say it does
dm_control: You, not the players, decide if and when Initiative is rolled
Some characters have abilities that trigger on Initiative roll
Players cannot force Initiative by their actions alone
rolling_initiative.example: A high-level Barbarian can't just punch their Paladin friend and roll Initiative to regain expended uses of Rage

advantage_on_initiative.when: When a character's actions initiate combat
advantage_on_initiative.grant: You can give the acting character Advantage on their Initiative roll
example: Sorcerer targets it with Chromatic Orb spell
Doppelganger might still act first if rolls well, reflecting its ability to anticipate spell

using_initiative_scores.title: Using Initiative Scores Instead of Rolling
using_initiative_scores.overview: You can get to combat action more quickly by using Initiative scores instead of rolling
flexibility: Use Initiative scores just for characters
Use Initiative scores just for monsters

implementation: Have players record Initiative scores on character sheets
Keep your own list of those scores

initiative_scores_for_monsters.location: Monster's stat block includes Initiative score after Initiative bonus

advantage_disadvantage_adjustments.condition: Creature has Advantage on Initiative rolls
Creature has Disadvantage on Initiative rolls

tracking_initiative.title: Tracking Initiative - Methods
tracking_initiative.overview: Different methods for keeping track of who goes when in combat

hidden_initiative_list.title: Hidden List
hidden_initiative_list.overview: Track Initiative on a list your players can't see

tools.description: One for each character and group of identical monsters, placed in Initiative order in a stack you cycle through

benefits: Track combatants who haven't been revealed yet
Record current Hit Points of monsters

usage_tips.tip: Tell players when their character's turn is starting
usage_tips.enhancement: When calling out the character whose turn is starting, also mention who's next
usage_tips.effect: Prompts next character's player to think ahead

open_initiative_list.title: Open List
open_initiative_list.overview: Track Initiative on a list visible to all players

tools.description: On wall or propped up nearby
Folded like tents for each character/monster group, placed across top of DM screen
Magnets, clothespins, or designed accessory representing Initiative order

benefits: Everyone aware of order of play
Players know when their turns are coming up
Players can plan actions in advance
open_initiative_list.monster_reveal: You can hold off on adding monsters to list until they take their first turns

tracking_monster_hit_points.title: Tracking Monsters' Hit Points
tracking_monster_hit_points.overview: During combat, track damage each monster takes
tracking_monster_hit_points.general_practice: Most DMs track damage in secret so players don't know remaining HP

system_for_groups.description: Need a system to track damage for groups of identical monsters

record_format.note: Can add names and descriptions to help track which monster is which

using_miniatures.differentiation: Give each identical miniature a unique tag
using_miniatures.example: Three ogres tagged A, B, C

hp_tracking_format.note: Cross out each number as damage is applied

damage_tracking_format.method_2: Track cumulative damage (add instead of subtract)
benefit: Can track on visible list without revealing total HP

using_and_tracking_conditions.title: Using and Tracking Conditions
using_and_tracking_conditions.overview: Rules and features apply conditions to creatures; you can also apply on the fly

flexibility.example: Poisoned condition can reflect various impairments

tracking_location: Track conditions where you track monster Hit Points
Players track conditions affecting their characters
Mark on index cards or whiteboard

physical_reminders.use: Hand to players when characters have condition
Place on player's character sheet for quick reference

physical_reminders.tip: Soda bottle cap rings work well

using_and_tracking_conditions.player_responsibility: Players should track any conditions affecting their characters
